description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:1.10.3 image with a docker:dind service and the DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay variable. Define three stages: build, release, and deploy. In the build stage add a build-container job that runs the global before_script to lowercase CI_PROJECT_PATH and export TEST_IMAGE_NAME, RELEASE_IMAGE_NAME, and IMAGE_NAME, then executes docker build -t $TEST_IMAGE_NAME ., docker login -e gitlab-ci@gitlab.com -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com, and docker push $TEST_IMAGE_NAME. Also in the build stage add a code-analysis-container job (except master) that uses the python:3 image and runs pip install 'coala-bears[alldeps]' followed by coala --ci. In the release stage add a release job that runs only on the master branch and performs docker login -e gitlab-ci@gitlab.com -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com, docker pull $TEST_IMAGE_NAME, docker tag $TEST_IMAGE_NAME $RELEASE_IMAGE_NAME, and docker push $RELEASE_IMAGE_NAME. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that runs only on master, with its own before_script installing openssh-client, creating ~/.ssh, writing $DEPLOYMENT_SERVER_PRIVATE_KEY to a private key file, setting permissions, starting ssh-agent, adding the key, scanning the deployment server IP, recomputing RELEASE_IMAGE_NAME and IMAGE_NAME, and then runs an ssh command to the server to docker login, pull $RELEASE_IMAGE_NAME, tag it as $IMAGE_NAME, and push $IMAGE_NAME.
